claim_boundary: Revalidation/ranking artifact only; no G005 success claim and no FDM-1 parity claim.

diagnostic_artifacts.claim_boundary: target_autocorr is a temporal-offset diagnostic/oracle over ground-truth targets, not a trained model result and not valid G005 completion evidence.
diagnostic_artifacts.name: nep_offset_target_autocorr_prefix320k

diagnostic_artifacts.claim_boundary: Materialization/code-path gates only; no trained-model metric evidence and no G005 success claim.

native_luma_gate.claim_boundary: Materialization throughput gate only. This does not claim G005 success, D2E paper-target metric win, or FDM-1 parity.
native_luma_gate.next_action: Use native-luma tensor-cache mode for a bounded 320k frozen-DINO prefix materialization/training gate before any full-corpus DINO extraction.

diagnostic_artifacts.claim_boundary: Prefix metric diagnostic only; no G005 completion evidence.

ranked_candidates.note: Best full-corpus mouse/key/button balance but audit flags event-state-context leakage/no closed-loop evidence and misses all paper targets.
Similar mouse Pearson but worse FPR and only world_size=1 metadata.
Invalid/promotional-risk state-token metric uses empty_bins_as_correct=true and high strict FPR; useful only for state-token diagnostics.
Good strict F1/FPR but weak paper keyboard/mouse and same no closed-loop context issue.
Non-leaky video path with bounded FPR but near-zero paper metrics; not viable alone.
Released G-IDM timing-correct infrastructure pilot over 200 rows, not our IDM and not exact-split/full baseline.
Full-corpus compact visual residual misses all paper targets and FPR gate.
Rejected 2GPU prefix; longer training did not rescue raw112 offset2 architecture.
Rejected 2GPU prefix: context dropout did not repair closed-loop distribution shift and caused severe mouse-button overfire.

top_audit_findings.details: All paper-compatible keyboard/button/mouse targets remain far below target.
Strict no-button false-positive rate increased to 0.7219 overall and ~1.0 on heldout_game/heldout_recording.
35% context dropout failed to repair closed-loop distribution shift and worsened FPR relative to the previous closed-loop prefix diagnostic.

ranked_candidates.note: Rejected 1GPU prefix: chronological target materialization ruled out ordering as root cause; no-button FPR is bounded but key/button/mouse metrics are far below paper targets.

top_audit_findings.details: The retest used a materialized 320k target prefix with zero per-recording timestamp violations and reproduced the same poor closed-loop prefix metrics; target row ordering is not the main failure mode.
Keyboard, mouse-button, and mouse-move paper-compatible metrics remain far below D2E paper targets.
No-button FPR is below 0.10, but this comes with extremely low button/key recall and strict mouse-button F1 0.0073.
Pivot to endpoint-specialist / mixture heads and calibrated decoding instead of more global closed-loop ordering/dropout retests.

ranked_candidates.note: Rejected CPU prefix matrix: event-context alone beat all tested state-luma endpoint mixtures; no mixture approaches paper key/button targets.

top_audit_findings.details: Among event/state endpoint mixtures, event-context alone had the best mouse-button accuracy and satisfied no-button FPR<=0.10.
State-luma button source raised no-button FPR to 0.1447 and gated/intersection variants reduced button accuracy below event-context alone.
Best prefix policy still has keyboard 0.1990, button 0.1726, Pearson X/Y 0.6048/0.5986, below paper targets.

ranked_candidates.note: Rejected 1GPU prefix: NEP-style future luma windows improved mouse scale but collapsed keyboard/button endpoint accuracy and failed heldout_game FPR gate.

top_audit_findings.details: Keyboard 0.0190 and mouse-button 0.0307 remain far below paper targets 0.730/0.957; mouse Pearson improves only to X/Y 0.5804/0.5546.
Adding 0/2/4/6/8 compact-luma future offsets to event-state-duration context did not improve endpoint classification; it is worse than the previous event-context prefix matrix on keyboard/button/F1.
Overall no-button FPR is 0.0764, but heldout_game FPR is 0.1073, above the <=0.10 hard gate.
Prefix materialization/stats used huge JSON rows and 1GPU run averaged only 0.184% GPU utilization; future probes should use tensor/cache features or CPU precompute before reserving GPUs.

ranked_candidates.note: Rejected 1GPU prefix threshold sweep: global key/button threshold lowering overfires buttons and is far worse than calibrated event_all.

top_audit_findings.details: Best global group threshold combo (key0p05_button0p1) has keyboard 0.0049, button 0.0128, and no-button FPR 0.8884; it is far worse than event_all and misses paper targets.
Lowering button thresholds to 0.05/0.10 causes no-button FPR 0.888-0.918, so recall cannot be recovered by simple global threshold lowering under the <=0.10 FPR gate.
Mouse Pearson X is high (0.8002) but Y remains below paper target (0.6427); category thresholds do not address the remaining mouse Y gap.
Inference-only sweep averaged 0.194% GPU utilization; future threshold diagnostics should run in a memory-rich CPU pod or use batched logit export rather than repeated JSON prediction.

ranked_candidates.note: Rejected learned hierarchical exact-set heads: safe no-button FPR, but worse than event_all prefix on keyboard, button, and mouse Pearson; misses every paper target.
Rejected causal held-key duration repeat/release priors: best keyboard 0.1978 (code_hold_mod_pressrelease_th0.3) is below event_all prefix keyboard and far below paper target.
Rejected prefix-only causal action-history transformer: no-button FPR is within gate, but keyboard/button collapse and motion remains below paper targets.
Rejected 2xH200 frozen-DINO compact-luma prefix: materialization/training completed, but keyboard/button and mouse Pearson collapsed; heldout_game no-button FPR exceeded 0.10.

Rejected compact visual-transition hash features for the learned repeat-key diagnostic: aligned 50k prefix improved over base but underperformed the non-visual hash gate and remains far below paper keyboard target.

Rejected top-16 vocabulary binary key specialist: with non-held key candidates included, base_all is best on the aligned 50k prefix and every specialist union/replacement policy reduces keyboard accuracy.

Rejected simply scaling the held-only learned hash specialist to 2M train rows: aligned 320k eval keyboard drops to 0.2222 versus the prior 320k-train/e2 gate 0.2337 and remains far below paper target.

Rejected mouse-button hash sequence specialist: on the aligned 50k prefix base_all remains best at button=0.1614, while the best specialist policy drops to 0.0805 and exceeds/approaches the no-button FPR gate.

recommended_next_actions: Do not promote frozen DINO-v2 compact-luma embeddings to full-corpus G005: the 320k 2xH200 prefix trained/evaluated successfully but achieved keyboard=0.0206, mouse-button=0.1046, Pearson X/Y=0.0199/0.0102 and heldout_game no-button FPR=0.1096.
Keep the native-luma tensor-cache materialization path as infrastructure only; it solves throughput/cache issues but not the IDM paper-target metric problem.

Key-event taxonomy shows the dominant keyboard blocker is held-repeat/same-bin key decoding: only 36.5% of key tokens are visible to prior->next state differencing, while an oracle repeat/tap key decoder plus state-delta buttons reaches keyboard=0.7418 and button=0.9754 on the 320k prefix. Next candidate should train a non-leaky key-repeat/tap specialist and pair it with state-transition button decoding and improved motion, not another frozen visual embedding alone.

A simple train-prefix repeat/release lookup specialist is now rejected: on a 50k aligned prefix it reports zero base/target sequence mismatches, but base_all remains best (keyboard=0.1550, button=0.1614, Pearson X/Y=0.7598/0.6794) and every repeat-table replacement/union policy reduces keyboard. Next key branch needs a learned sequence model or teacher/distillation signal, not tabular hold-age thresholds.

The learned hashed key specialist is the first repeat-key branch with a real aligned-prefix gain: on 320k rows, press-only union improves keyboard from 0.1990 to 0.2337 with zero sequence mismatches while preserving button/motion from the base stream. It is still far below paper keyboard=0.73 and motion-y target, so continue with a stronger neural/teacher-assisted sequence model rather than checkpointing G005.

Compact visual-transition hash features are rejected for the learned repeat-key branch: on the aligned 50k prefix, best keyboard=0.1950 underperforms the non-visual hash gate keyboard=0.1993 and remains far below paper keyboard=0.73; do not reserve GPUs for this exact visual-hash specialist.

Top-key vocabulary binary specialist is rejected: on the aligned 50k prefix, base_all remains best at keyboard=0.1550 while the best topvocab specialist policy reaches only 0.1123 and is worse than the held-only hash gate 0.1993; do not reserve GPUs for this branch.

Scaling the held-only learned hash specialist to 2M train rows is rejected: aligned 320k keyboard=0.2222, below the prior 320k/e2 hash gate 0.2337 and far below paper target; future work needs a new sequence-state or teacher-assisted mechanism, not more rows for this hash model.

Phase-aware held-key repeat heuristics are also rejected: on the aligned 50k prefix the best phase policy improves keyboard by only +0.00026 over base_all (0.15505 -> 0.15530), far below paper target. Do not spend GPU on handcrafted repeat phase features; next branch needs learned sequence/teacher-assisted supervision or released-GIDM distillation.

Mouse-button hash sequence specialist is rejected: on the aligned 50k prefix, base_all remains best at button=0.1614, while the best specialist drops to 0.0805 and risks no-button FPR >0.10; button endpoint needs a different learned head/teacher signal.

Do not reserve H200s for threshold-only global decoding, context-dropout, chronological closed-loop, endpoint mixtures, naive luma windows, hierarchical exact-set heads, causal repeat priors, sequence-prior action history, or frozen-DINO-only prefix runs; all miss paper targets under empty_bins_as_correct=false.

Avoid repeated JSON prediction sweeps on GPUs; export logits once or run diagnostics in memory-rich CPU contexts before reserving H200s. Precompute action-history stats/cache outside GPU reservations when possible.
